fields.description:
## Summary
A `confirmed` binding whose Jira issue was deleted AND whose local ticket was archived is never garbage-collected — it persists in `bindings.json` forever. Proven: `f8b5-2f30 -> REB-530` is `confirmed`, but REB-530 no longer exists in Jira and f8b5 is locally archived.

## Root Cause (binding GC gated on the local ticket being live)
- The only path that retires a binding for a vanished Jira issue is the "bound-but-absent" direct-GET probe in `outbound_differ.py:598-611` -> `binding_store.note_absent` -> retire after GRACE consecutive 404s (`binding_store.py:292-337`).
- That probe only runs for NON-excluded local tickets: archived/deleted are skipped at `outbound_differ.py:471` (candidate pre-selection) and `:539` (main loop), `excluded_statuses={archived,deleted}`. So an archived-local binding is never probed -> REB-530 is never GET'd -> never `note_absent` -> never retired.
- `recover_pending_bindings` scans only `pending` bindings (`binding_store.py:384`), never `confirmed`. There is no other path that validates a confirmed binding's Jira side still exists.
- Runtime proof: `reconcile --mode dry-run` plans nothing for f8b5/REB-530.

## In plain language
A ticket was synced to a Jira issue (a link record: f8b5 <-> REB-530). Later BOTH sides disappeared — the ticket was archived locally and the Jira issue was deleted — but the link record stays, marked "confirmed", forever. It lingers because the code that retires a stale link works by fetching the Jira issue and waiting for repeated 404s, and that fetch only runs for links whose local ticket is still active. Since the ticket is archived, the reconciler skips it, never fetches REB-530, never sees the 404, never retires the link. The separate "recover" routine only cleans up half-created (pending) links, never confirmed ones.

## Implications
- Unbounded leak: `bindings.json` accumulates dead entries; invisible because `bridge_fsck` cannot see them either (class-D ticket).
- Latent correctness hazard: a stale binding still claims a Jira key. If the ticket is un-archived, or the key is reused, outbound updates target a nonexistent issue (404s) or a clean re-create is blocked.
- Current real impact is low (one test ticket), but the leak grows and carries a resurrection hazard.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Sync a local ticket to Jira (confirmed binding).
2. Delete the Jira issue AND archive the local ticket.
3. Run reconcile passes; observe the binding stays `confirmed` in `.tickets-tracker/.bridge_state/bindings.json`.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: a confirmed binding whose Jira issue no longer exists is retired (unbound), even when the local ticket is archived/deleted.
- Actual: the binding persists indefinitely.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A confirmed binding whose Jira issue is gone is retired within a bounded number of passes even when the local ticket is archived/deleted.
- [ ] Binding GC is driven from the binding store (bounded by #bindings), unified with the class-A terminal-transition detection.
- [ ] A RED test: confirmed binding to a nonexistent Jira key + archived local ticket -> asserts retirement; RED before fix.
- [ ] f8b5-2f30 -> REB-530 is retired after the fix.

## Notes
Discovered comparing Jira REB vs local. Files: `outbound_differ.py`, `binding_store.py`, `reconcile.py`.

## Guardrails (must-preserve — prior-art + archaeology; folds in the corrected plan)
- [ ] GC a confirmed binding ONLY from a bounded direct-GET 404 counted to `RECONCILER_ABSENT_RETIRE_GRACE` consecutive misses (L13/L14) — NEVER from snapshot-absence (L17: Done-beyond-`_DONE_RECENT_CAP` issues are alive-but-absent; absence-keyed GC would mass-retire the whole Done backlog).
- [ ] Retirement stays REVERSIBLE (soft-delete to `bindings-retired.json`); a 200 GET resets the counter; interacts safely with `recover_pending_bindings` (L15).
- [ ] Extend the existing archived-gated probe so an archived/deleted local ticket's binding IS probed (the current skip at `outbound_differ.py:471,539` is the root cause) — unified with class A's binding-store loop.
- [ ] Circuit breaker backstops a mass-retire from any transient fetch/JQL-scope regression [prior art: Argo `allowEmpty`].

## Implementation contract (foundation-first — read before coding)
DEPENDS ON amiss-bun-wince (baf7): this bug is the `PROBE_GET` -> `RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE` Decision chain of `classify()`, driven from the binding-store walk — the SAME walk as bug 444d (class A); implement them against one shared iteration, not two loops.
1. The classifier routes bound+ABSENT_IN_WINDOW to PROBE_GET regardless of local state — the fix is precisely that an archived/deleted local no longer exempts its binding from the probe (today's skip at the excluded_statuses candidate filter in outbound_differ.py is the root cause).
2. REUSE the existing probe machinery UNCHANGED: `_safe_get_issue` sentinels (_DELETED/_TRANSPORT_ERROR), bounded GET budget with oldest-first rotation, `note_absent`/`clear_absent` grace counting (RECONCILER_ABSENT_RETIRE_GRACE, default 3), reversible retirement to bindings-retired.json. Your change is WHICH pairs enter the probe set, not HOW probing works.
3. ADR 0028 #1 is absolute: ABSENT_IN_WINDOW never retires directly (the Done-beyond-_DONE_RECENT_CAP backlog is alive-but-absent — a naive absent==gone would mass-retire it; that is the exact regression cell test to write). Only CONFIRMED_404 counted to grace yields RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE.
4. RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE is an acting kind counted by the circuit breaker; a fetch/JQL regression that empties the window must trip the breaker BEFORE mass-retire (test this cell: snapshot={} -> breaker refuses).
RED test: confirmed binding to a nonexistent key + archived local -> probe fires -> grace counts across 3 simulated passes -> retirement (reversible; entry lands in bindings-retired.json). Regression cells: Done-beyond-cap NOT retired; transport-error defers without incrementing grace.
Live acceptance cell: f8b5-2f30 -> REB-530 retired after grace (REB-530 confirmed absent from the 2026-07-03 snapshot; bindings-retired.json has NEVER existed on prod — its first-ever write is itself a verification point for the commit path in _commit_binding_store_snapshot).
Files: classify.py (cells), outbound_differ.py (probe-set selection), binding_store.py (consume grace/retire as-is), tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/classify/ + state/.
